spoken_briefing:
This week in AI tools for specialty contractors and small general contractors.

Starting with bidding and estimating. STACK Construction Technologies named a new CEO, Viyas Sundaram, in March 2026 to lead its next chapter of AI innovation, with the platform crossing thirty-one point eight million dollars in annual revenue. STACK remains a leading cloud-based estimating platform for trade subcontractors, moving teams from uploaded plans to bid-ready numbers fast. Pricing starts at two thousand nine hundred ninety-nine dollars per year, and it connects with Procore for budget and cost code synchronization.

Togal dot AI offers three pricing tiers for 2026: Essential at one hundred ninety-nine dollars per user per month, Growth at two hundred ninety-nine dollars per user per month billed annually, and a Business tier on custom quote for teams of four or more. Its AI-driven floor plan takeoffs claim ninety-eight percent accuracy, with estimators completing full architectural takeoffs in twelve minutes instead of hours. The platform features a three D visualization tool, AI-powered image search, and TogalCHAT for querying project documents. Coastal Construction documented saving fourteen and a half hours per plan set, adding up to roughly one million dollars in annual cost savings. NC Painting used it to scale to sixty bids per month in under sixty days.

BidIntell is a pre-bid decision platform built specifically for specialty subcontractors who need to filter which invitations are worth pursuing. It scores incoming bid requests on a one-hundred-point BidIndex based on location fit, contract terms, your relationship history with the GC, and trade match. Pricing starts at forty-nine dollars per user per month, making it one of the most accessible AI tools on the market right now.

Beam AI from Attentive dot AI raised thirty point five million dollars in a Series B round in November 2025, bringing total funding to forty-eight million dollars and validating the platform's momentum with more than eleven hundred contractors and suppliers on board. The platform combines automated quantity takeoffs with human review to ensure accuracy across HVAC, electrical, plumbing, mechanical, and civil trades, and is now expanding beyond takeoffs into the full preconstruction lifecycle including estimating and bidding workflows. Pricing is custom-quoted based on volume.

Downtobid is an AI-powered bid management platform that reads construction plans, identifies every trade scope automatically, and sends personalized bid invitations to verified local subcontractors from a database of more than two hundred fifty thousand commercial subs. General contractors start at one hundred forty-nine dollars per month, and subcontractors accessing the full platform pay two hundred ninety-nine dollars per month. Personalized invites using the platform generate two times more responses than generic portal emails.

ConstructConnect Takeoff Boost is an AI-powered takeoff service built on Google Cloud using Gemini and NVIDIA computer vision. It automatically classifies, detects, counts, and measures areas, linears, and objects directly from digital plan sets and converts them into structured measurements in seconds. Available now to ConstructConnect customers through OnScreen Takeoff with PlanSwift integration coming. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Moving to submittal and RFI tracking. Trunk Tools expanded its Autodesk Forma integration in May 2026, adding a full suite of agents including bid analysis, SOP adherence, and scope writing on top of its existing RFI management and submittal review capabilities, all available directly to project teams running Autodesk Forma Build. Its three core products remain TrunkSubmittal for spec-matched submittal review, TrunkText for conversational queries across project documents, and TrunkReview for analyzing changes in newly issued drawing sets. Cleveland Construction reported saving more than seven hundred ninety hours and sixty thousand dollars across four projects. Pricing is by custom quote.

Procore's AI platform, Procore Helix, continues expanding in 2026 following its Datagrid acquisition in January 2026. On May twenty-first, Procore launched native AI agents including Deep Search, a Daily Log agent, a Submittals agent, an RFI agent, and a Contract Review agent. At CONEXPO and Groundbreak 2026, Procore also unveiled an enhanced Resource view giving general contractors and specialty contractors better workforce planning tools, and a redesigned submittal experience with automated deadline calculations and a new centralized Submittal Plan card that eliminates manual date entry. The embedded AI experience is in private beta with broader availability expected this summer. Procore is priced on annual construction volume.

Civils dot AI extracts data directly from drawings, specs, and building codes to automate RFI generation and compliance tracking. It is built for both GC and specialty sub teams, and pricing is by custom quote.

BuildSync is a purpose-built AI submittal review platform that compares submitted product data against project specifications with ninety-five percent accuracy. It cuts first-submission rejection rates from thirty-five percent to five percent and reduces review time by seventy to eighty percent. BuildSync integrates directly with Procore and Autodesk Construction Cloud. Pricing is custom-quoted.

SubmittalLink is a platform purpose-built for subcontractors managing construction submittals and RFIs on an accessible subscription model. It converts spec books into structured submittal logs automatically, sends notification-driven routing across the team, and uses AI to parse drawings, generate status reports, and automate coversheet creation. All plans include unlimited users, projects, and file storage. Pricing starts at one hundred fifty dollars per month for teams with under five million dollars in annual construction volume, and two hundred fifty dollars per month for five to twenty-five million dollar teams.

New in submittal and RFI tracking is Constructable, an AI-native field and project management platform built for mid-market general contractors running twenty million to one hundred fifty million dollar commercial projects. Constructable pins RFIs directly to the drawing location where questions arise, uses an AI Answer Engine to pull answers from past project data instantly, and includes unlimited users on all plans with no per-seat fees and no overage charges. The platform covers drawings, RFIs, submittals, punch lists, and financial management in one system and onboards teams in under twenty-one days. Pricing starts at eight hundred fifty dollars per month for field management, two thousand dollars per month for field and project management, and three thousand five hundred dollars per month for the complete field, project, and financial management tier.

Now for field management and daily reporting. CompanyCam is a photo-first field documentation platform that achieved unicorn status after closing a Series C funding round in August 2025. Its AI Walkthrough Note feature lets a superintendent walk the site, speak observations, and snap photos, and AI automatically assembles a structured daily report. CompanyCam acquired Beam Finance in March 2026 to add AI estimating, payments, and invoicing to the platform. Plans start at ninety-nine dollars per month with a three-user minimum.

eSUB is the subcontractor-exclusive field management platform that has added meaningful AI capabilities in 2026, including automated daily report drafting from field supervisor voice notes, predictive labor cost analysis that flags tasks trending over budget, and intelligent RFI drafting that suggests relevant specification sections. A mechanical sub using the AI daily report drafting feature reduced foreman paperwork from forty-five minutes to ten minutes per day. Pricing is by custom quote.

Buildots has added major new construction firms to its customer roster in 2026, now including STO Building Group, Mortenson, Ledcor, and Pomerleau alongside existing clients Turner Construction, JE Dunn, Digital Realty, and Intel. The platform uses helmet-mounted cameras and computer vision to capture real-time site progress against the schedule. Total funding reached one hundred sixty-six million dollars on a forty-five million dollar Series D. Pricing is custom-quoted by project size.

Raken received a majority stake investment from Sverica Capital in September 2025, giving the field reporting platform new private equity backing to accelerate product development and market expansion. Raken now serves more than forty-five hundred construction firms and seventy thousand users with daily reports, time tracking, toolbox talks, and production tracking. Pricing starts at fifteen dollars per user per month for the Lite plan and twenty-five dollars per user per month for Pro.

BuildOps launched its Projects module in May 2026, giving specialty contractors an AI-native system to run multi-month construction work from kickoff to closeout alongside their service operations. Powered by OpsAI for scheduling, document management, field coordination, and daily reporting. BuildOps raised one hundred twenty-seven million dollars in a Series C round in 2026, reaching a one billion dollar valuation. Pricing is by custom quote.

InspectMind AI is a Y Combinator-backed platform that generates field reports, punch lists, safety inspections, and site walkthroughs in minutes. Its Field Reports product captures photos and voice notes on mobile, works offline, and produces structured reports at one hundred dollars per user per month. Plan checking starts at one hundred dollars per review. It integrates with Procore and Autodesk Construction Cloud.

iFieldSmart from iFieldSmart Technologies is a construction management platform with reality capture and AI-powered field documentation. Its Lens three-sixty feature turns a single site walkthrough into a complete digital twin. Pricing starts at fourteen dollars and ninety-nine cents per user per month.

Kahua is a flexible construction management platform with built-in AI through its Noa assistant, which automates repetitive project workflows, uncovers insights from project data, and lets teams build custom apps in minutes. Pricing is custom-quoted with implementation costs typically ranging from five thousand to fifty thousand dollars or more.

For time and materials and billing automation. HCSS HeavyJob is the go-to standard for heavy and civil specialty contractors doing time-and-material work. It pulls T and M billing data directly from field time cards and generates accurate invoices with minimal manual effort. Pricing ranges from sixty to over four thousand dollars per user per year depending on modules selected.

FOUNDATION Software's T and M Billing Module captures every billable expense, automates markups, and produces client-ready invoices for time-and-material contracts. Pricing is custom-quoted.

SmartBarrel combines AI biometric facial recognition for time tracking with PPE compliance monitoring at clock-in, giving specialty subs a dual-purpose tool. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Rhumbix is a mobile-first time and materials tracking platform that automates T and M ticket generation directly from field time cards and captures unplanned work in real time. It integrates with Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, QuickBooks Online, and ADP. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Siteline is the billing and pay application platform built exclusively for commercial specialty subcontractors. It automates AIA pay app preparation, lien waiver collection, and vendor compliance tracking. Customers bill six times faster and reduce invoice aging by thirty percent on average. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Adaptive is an AI-native construction accounting platform that deploys AI agents to automate manual accounting work and deliver real-time financial control. Pricing starts at five hundred seventy-five dollars per month for businesses with up to five million dollars in annual revenue.

SubBase is an AI-powered materials procurement platform for specialty subcontractors that uses AI to automatically compare purchase orders, invoices, and delivery tickets for three-way reconciliation, completing the process in an average of twenty-one seconds compared to three to five minutes of manual work. Since the feature launched, SubBase has processed more than four thousand invoices covering over three and a half million dollars in purchases. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Payra is a B2B accounts receivable automation platform built for construction contractors running on legacy ERP systems, raising fifteen million dollars from Edison Partners in February 2026. It integrates directly with Trimble Viewpoint, FOUNDATION, Sage, and others to automate cash application and payment reconciliation without replacing existing systems. Customers have documented a twenty percent reduction in days sales outstanding and a seventy-five percent drop in past-due invoices. Pricing is transaction-based.

Intuit launched its Enterprise Suite Construction Edition on February eleventh, 2026, delivering a purpose-built AI-powered ERP for mid-market contractors. It includes project phases with AI-enabled cost group tracking, AIA-style invoicing, negative change orders, proposal tools with e-signatures, and an AI Project Management Agent for cash flow and profitability monitoring. It is currently in open beta at no additional cost for existing Intuit Enterprise Suite customers, and available as a paid add-on for QuickBooks Online Advanced.

On change order and document management. Document Crunch is the AI contract review platform that reads construction contracts, specs, and insurance documents and surfaces risk clauses, change order triggers, and compliance obligations in plain language. Trimble announced its acquisition of Document Crunch on April 2, 2026, with the deal expected to close in the second quarter of 2026 and full integration planned into the Trimble Construction One ecosystem. The platform now serves more than four hundred customers processing three hundred fifty billion dollars in construction volume across ten thousand project kickoffs.

Procore's change order management with AI Assist lets project teams identify scope gaps, draft change order language, and track financial impacts in real time. Pricing is on annual construction volume.

Pelles AI is a generative AI platform that creates RFIs, submittal logs, and proposals in your company's existing format, giving smaller teams the output speed of a much larger document production operation. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Clearstory has had a major 2026 with significant new deployments and product updates. Suffolk Construction named Clearstory the standard for change order management across its national portfolio in March 2026, joining Turner Construction which has deployed the platform on more than three hundred fifty projects. New AI features include AI Reason Categories, which automatically classifies change orders into root cause categories for change order intelligence; Bulk AI Import, which brings entire COR backlogs into the system with AI-driven field mapping; a new Sage Intacct integration; and Procore Contract Mapping for automatic contract data population. Specialty contractors get change orders approved up to seventy-five percent faster and cut processing time by seventy percent or more. The basic tier is free for all stakeholders.

Now for proposal generation and pre-bid analysis. BidIntell's pre-bid scoring anchors the proposal phase, helping specialty subs invest estimating hours only on the invitations that fit their win profile before a single takeoff is started. Pricing starts at forty-nine dollars per user per month.

Flowcase is an AI proposal management platform that matches company experience and employee qualifications to RFP requirements and auto-populates bid templates. Especially strong for design-assist and qualifications-based work. Pricing is custom-quoted.

ContraVault takes a team from opportunity qualification to a submission-ready proposal using AI to assemble, draft, and review content. Built for GC and specialty sub teams competing for complex awards. A 2025 Dodge Construction Network survey found that average bid preparation time has dropped from thirty-four hours to fourteen hours per project for contractors using AI-powered estimating, and those contractors reported nineteen percent fewer cost overruns and twenty-two percent fewer change orders traced to estimating errors.

On project scheduling with AI. ALICE Technologies formalized a partnership with McKinsey and Company in April 2026 to bring generative scheduling to capital project clients across infrastructure, data centers, and energy, introducing the technology to more than thirty-five clients with schedule accelerations of up to twenty percent. A McKinsey data center client reduced its entire construction schedule by forty percent. The platform's Schedule Insights Agent allows teams to interact with their schedule using natural language. Earlier documented results include a seventeen percent reduction in project duration, fourteen percent labor cost savings, and twelve percent equipment cost savings. Pricing is by custom quote.

Phoenix Project Manager is built for specialty contractors who need resource-loaded critical path schedules without the complexity of Primavera P six. Its labor histogram view lets electrical, mechanical, and plumbing contractors see workforce demand across their entire project portfolio. Starting at eighteen hundred dollars per year.

Oracle Primavera P six remains the industry standard on large complex projects at roughly twenty-one hundred to twenty-five hundred dollars per user per year, and its AI capabilities are being extended through ALICE and third-party integrations.

nPlan announced a partnership with the UK highways sector in February 2026, further extending its AI scheduling capabilities trained on seven hundred fifty thousand historical schedules representing over two trillion dollars of construction spend. The company raised sixteen million dollars in a Series B in October 2025, bringing total funding to thirty-six and a half million dollars. Products include Schedule Studio, Barry the AI decision assistant, nPlan Insights Pro, and nPlan Portfolio. Pricing is by custom quote.

SmartPM is an AI-driven automated project controls and schedule analytics platform that integrates with existing CPM scheduling software to flag delays and critical path risks in real time. Total funding is thirteen and a half million dollars. Pricing starts at four hundred dollars per project per month.

Outbuild launched Project Intelligence in April 2026, adding a mobile field app for superintendents and foremen, an AI Assistant for natural language schedule queries, and an executive dashboard for portfolio-wide visibility. Trusted by four hundred or more customers managing over fifty billion dollars in active projects. Pricing scales based on annual construction volume.

Planera is an AI-powered CPM scheduling platform with unlimited-user collaboration and project-based pricing. It raised twenty-six and a half million dollars in total funding and has experienced six hundred percent growth in six months. Pricing is project-based. Integrates with Oracle Primavera P six.

Finally, safety and compliance monitoring. Intenseye uses existing camera infrastructure to detect more than fifty categories of unsafe acts and conditions in real time. Named a World Economic Forum Global Innovator in February 2026. Integrated NVIDIA Cosmos Reason 2 in March 2026 to advance vision AI capabilities and launched Intenseye Chief, an EHS-trained AI assistant that turns safety data into actionable insights. Total funding is approximately ninety-four million dollars. Pricing by custom quote based on camera count.

Voxel AI offers AI-powered safety monitoring at an estimated thirty to fifty percent lower cost than comparable enterprise platforms. Total funding now at eighty million dollars including a forty-four million dollar Series B. Pricing structured around camera count.

Oracle launched its Construction and Engineering Advisor for Safety in March 2026, adding predictive analytics that forecast where safety incidents are likely before they happen. Built for large GC and owner teams running complex projects.

Pillar Technologies uses connected environmental sensors alongside visual monitoring to deliver real-time construction site risk management, with alerts covering temperature, moisture, atmospheric conditions, and visual hazard detection. Pricing is custom-quoted.

DroneDeploy Safety AI analyzes drone imagery and site camera data against OSHA standards to detect construction hazards including missing guardrails, exposed edges, and PPE violations. Beta customers reduced the occurrence of unsafe conditions by up to eighty-nine percent within three weeks. Pricing for the individual plan starts at three hundred forty-nine dollars per month billed annually.

viAct transforms existing site cameras into AI safety monitors using more than two hundred specialized detection modules. Pricing starts at two hundred dollars per camera per module per month, with a fourteen-day free trial.

Fyld raised forty-one million dollars in a Series B round in February 2026 and reported eighty-two percent year-over-year growth in 2025. Contractors report up to forty-eight percent reduction in serious workplace incidents. Customers include Kiewit, Quanta Services, and Emery Sapp and Sons. Pricing is by custom quote.

Safety Evolution's SE-AI platform replaces more than twenty hours of weekly administrative safety work with three AI agents covering compliance monitoring, workforce training management, and safety intelligence. It includes built-in support for COR and SECOR compliance and ISNetworld integration. Pricing is tiered per user.

Kwant dot AI is an AI-powered workforce management and safety platform that combines ATEX-certified smart wearables, IoT sensors, and AI analytics to automate safety orientation, track certifications, and detect incidents in real time. Pricing starts at approximately fifty thousand dollars for a single-site rollout including wearables and sensors, with custom pricing for portfolio deployments. It is built for both general contractors and specialty subcontractors.

New in safety and compliance monitoring is Hakimo AI, an AI-powered physical security and safety monitoring platform for construction jobsites. Skanska has deployed Hakimo AI to proactively monitor major infrastructure projects, using AI-driven camera surveillance to prevent unauthorized access, detect safety conditions, and flag incidents in real time. Hakimo raised ten and a half million dollars in a Series A in 2025, bringing total funding to twenty and a half million dollars. Customers have documented a ninety percent drop in incidents and security cost reductions of up to two-thirds compared to traditional guard services. Pricing is by custom quote based on camera count.

Now for market context. On what owners and large GCs are expecting: major general contractors are increasingly requiring specialty subcontractors to actively manage submittals, RFIs, and daily documentation inside Procore or Autodesk Build as a condition of project participation. BIM coordination and model-based submittals are expected on commercial projects above ten million dollars. Digital daily reports with photo documentation and real-time cost tracking are moving from nice-to-have to contractual obligation. Subs who cannot interface digitally with GC platforms are being filtered from bid lists before the first conversation happens. AI adoption in construction has now doubled year over year, with thirty-eight percent of contractors reporting measurable business impact from AI, up from seventeen percent just one year ago, and ninety-four percent of those using AI plan to increase their usage in 2026.

On realistic adoption cost for a specialty sub with five to twenty project managers: a functional AI stack covering estimating, field documentation, submittal management, and billing automation typically runs between fifteen thousand and eighty thousand dollars per year in software costs. Training represents an additional fifteen to twenty percent of the total investment and is the factor most consistently underestimated. Implementation typically takes sixty to ninety days before teams reach operational efficiency.

On documented ROI: a 2025 Bluebeam industry report found that sixty-eight percent of AI early adopters in construction have saved at least fifty thousand dollars, and forty-six percent have recovered between five hundred and one thousand hours of labor. The average return across the industry is three and a half times the investment, with top performers reporting ten times ROI. Bid preparation time is down by as much as sixty percent. Clark Construction documented three point two million dollars in project savings through AI-assisted risk management. Firms using AI scheduling report twenty to thirty percent fewer project delays. Payra customers using AI accounts receivable automation document a twenty percent reduction in days sales outstanding and a seventy-five percent drop in past-due invoices. The message for specialty subs is clear: the tools are commercially available today, the ROI is documented and growing, and the window to adopt before digital capability becomes a competitive prerequisite is closing fast.

tools.name: Procore AI (Helix / Agent Builder)
tools.description: Procore Helix intelligence layer significantly expanded in 2026. Procore acquired Datagrid, a vertical agentic AI platform, in January 2026. On May 21, 2026, launched native AI agents: Deep Search (answers across specs, drawings, and RFIs), Daily Log Agent, Submittals Agent, RFI Agent, and Contract Review Agent. At CONEXPO and Groundbreak 2026, Procore also released an enhanced Resource view for specialty contractor workforce planning and a redesigned submittal experience with automated deadline calculations and a centralized Submittal Plan card. Also launched Procore Scheduling in February 2026, now adopted by over 2,000 companies and the fastest-adopted product in Procore's history. Agents use Actions to execute live steps inside Procore and Triggers to respond automatically to project events. Currently in private beta; broader availability expected summer 2026. Also includes Agent Builder for no-code custom agents, Photo AI, and multilingual Procore Assist. Q1 2026 revenue was $359M (+15.7% YoY) with 2,795 enterprise customers ($100K+ ARR).
tools.pricing: Included in Procore platform; priced on Annual Construction Volume (ACV)
tools.results: Serves 17,500+ customers. Q1 2026 revenue $359M, +15.7% YoY. Datagrid acquisition January 2026. New AI agents launched May 21, 2026. Procore Scheduling launched February 2026: 2,000+ companies adopted, fastest-adopted product in Procore history. CONEXPO/Groundbreak 2026: enhanced Resource view; redesigned submittal experience. NVIDIA Omniverse DSX Blueprint integration announced.
tools.integrations: Autodesk, Sage, Viewpoint, Trimble, 400+ third-party apps; Datagrid multimodal index across drawings, specs, photos; NVIDIA Omniverse DSX Blueprint
tools.spoken: Procore's AI platform, Procore Helix, continues expanding in 2026 following its Datagrid acquisition in January 2026. On May twenty-first, Procore launched native AI agents including Deep Search, a Daily Log agent, a Submittals agent, an RFI agent, and a Contract Review agent. At CONEXPO and Groundbreak 2026, Procore also unveiled an enhanced Resource view giving general contractors and specialty contractors better workforce planning tools, and a redesigned submittal experience with automated deadline calculations and a new centralized Submittal Plan card that eliminates manual date entry. The embedded AI experience is in private beta with broader availability expected this summer. Procore is priced on annual construction volume.

tools.name: Civils.ai
tools.description: AI platform that extracts data from drawings, specifications, and building codes to enable automated RFI generation, advanced search, and compliance workflow automation.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Reduces manual review time; catches compliance issues early to reduce RFI back-and-forth cycles.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with major project management platforms
tools.spoken: Civils dot AI extracts data directly from drawings, specs, and building codes to automate RFI generation and compliance tracking. It is built for both GC and specialty sub teams, and pricing is by custom quote.

tools.name: BuildSync
tools.description: Purpose-built AI submittal review platform that compares submitted product data against project specifications with 95%+ accuracy on technical compliance checking. Reduces first-submission rejection rates from 35% to 5% and cuts review time by 70-80%.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: 95%+ accuracy on technical compliance checking; cuts first-submission rejection rates from 35% to 5%; reduces review time 70-80%; each rejection costs $805 and adds 2-4 weeks.
tools.integrations: Procore (automated routing with compliance reports returned to Procore workflow); Autodesk Construction Cloud (two-way sync); API access
tools.spoken: BuildSync is a purpose-built AI submittal review platform that compares submitted product data against project specifications with ninety-five percent accuracy. It cuts first-submission rejection rates from thirty-five percent to five percent and reduces review time by seventy to eighty percent. BuildSync integrates directly with Procore and Autodesk Construction Cloud. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: SubmittalLink
tools.description: AI-powered submittal and RFI management platform purpose-built for subcontractors with transparent volume-based pricing and unlimited users on all plans. Converts spec books into structured submittal logs automatically, manages RFI routing with notification tracking, and uses AI to parse drawings, generate status reports, and automate coversheet creation. Mobile app enables field review. AI also powers high-level project health analytics and automated owner/developer status reports.
tools.pricing: $150/month (Starter, under $5M annual construction volume); $250/month (Pro, $5M-$25M); Enterprise custom quote above $25M. All plans include unlimited users, projects, and file storage.
tools.results: Unlimited users at no extra cost; AI automatically generates structured submittal logs from spec books; mobile access for field review; automated owner reporting.
tools.integrations: Standalone platform; mobile app for field access
tools.spoken: SubmittalLink is a platform purpose-built for subcontractors managing construction submittals and RFIs on an accessible subscription model. It converts spec books into structured submittal logs automatically, sends notification-driven routing across the team, and uses AI to parse drawings, generate status reports, and automate coversheet creation. All plans include unlimited users, projects, and file storage. Pricing starts at one hundred fifty dollars per month for teams with under five million dollars in annual construction volume, and two hundred fifty dollars per month for five to twenty-five million dollar teams.

tools.name: Constructable
tools.description: NEW. AI-native field and project management platform built for mid-market general contractors running $20M-$150M commercial projects. Pins RFIs directly to drawing locations with full context, uses an AI Answer Engine to pull answers from past project data instantly, and provides unlimited users on all plans with no per-seat fees, no ACV audits, and no overage charges. Covers drawings, RFIs, submittals, punch lists, and financial management (budgets, commitments, change orders, QuickBooks Online and Sage Intacct sync) in one system. Onboards teams in under 21 days.
tools.pricing: $850/month (Field Management); $2,000/month (Field and Project Management); $3,500/month (Field, Project, and Financial Management); flat monthly or annual billing; unlimited users included
tools.results: Unlimited users at flat rate; onboards teams in under 21 days; RFIs pinned to exact drawing locations for clear accountability; AI Answer Engine surfaces answers instantly from past project data.
tools.integrations: QuickBooks Online, Sage Intacct (financial sync); standalone platform for field and project management
tools.spoken: New in submittal and RFI tracking is Constructable, an AI-native field and project management platform built for mid-market general contractors running twenty million to one hundred fifty million dollar commercial projects. Constructable pins RFIs directly to the drawing location where questions arise, uses an AI Answer Engine to pull answers from past project data instantly, and includes unlimited users on all plans with no per-seat fees and no overage charges. The platform covers drawings, RFIs, submittals, punch lists, and financial management in one system and onboards teams in under twenty-one days. Pricing starts at eight hundred fifty dollars per month for field management, two thousand dollars per month for field and project management, and three thousand five hundred dollars per month for the complete field, project, and financial management tier.

tools.name: CompanyCam
tools.description: Photo-first field documentation platform that achieved unicorn status after a Series C funding round in August 2025. AI Walkthrough Note feature assembles structured daily reports from voice observations and site photos. Acquired Beam Finance in March 2026 to add AI estimating, payments, and invoicing capabilities.
tools.pricing: From $99/month (3-user minimum); Enterprise custom pricing
tools.results: Eliminates manual daily report typing. Achieved unicorn status (Series C, August 2025). Acquired Beam Finance (March 2026) to expand into financial management.
tools.integrations: Procore, JobNimbus, JobProgress, DroneDeploy, HOVER, SuccessWare 21
tools.spoken: CompanyCam is a photo-first field documentation platform that achieved unicorn status after closing a Series C funding round in August 2025. Its AI Walkthrough Note feature lets a superintendent walk the site, speak observations, and snap photos, and AI automatically assembles a structured daily report. CompanyCam acquired Beam Finance in March 2026 to add AI estimating, payments, and invoicing to the platform. Plans start at ninety-nine dollars per month with a three-user minimum.

tools.name: eSUB
tools.description: Mobile-first field management platform built exclusively for commercial specialty subcontractors. Added meaningful AI capabilities in 2026 including automated daily report drafting from field supervisor voice notes, predictive labor cost analysis flagging tasks trending over budget based on daily productivity data, and intelligent RFI drafting that suggests relevant specification sections.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; positioned as more affordable than Procore for sub-only teams
tools.results: AI daily report drafting reduced foreman paperwork from 45 minutes to 10 minutes per day for mechanical sub users. 95% of customers fully operational within 90 days.
tools.integrations: Sage 300 (integration released), major accounting platforms, drawing and design platforms
tools.spoken: eSUB is the subcontractor-exclusive field management platform that has added meaningful AI capabilities in 2026, including automated daily report drafting from field supervisor voice notes, predictive labor cost analysis that flags tasks trending over budget, and intelligent RFI drafting that suggests relevant specification sections. A mechanical sub using the AI daily report drafting feature reduced foreman paperwork from forty-five minutes to ten minutes per day. Pricing is by custom quote.

tools.name: Buildots
tools.description: AI construction intelligence platform using helmet-mounted cameras and computer vision to capture real-time site progress and compare against schedule. Raised $45M Series D (total $166M). In April 2026 launched the construction intelligence category. In 2026 added major new customers including STO Building Group, Mortenson, Ledcor, and Pomerleau to its North American roster alongside Turner Construction, JE Dunn, Digital Realty, and Intel.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on project size
tools.results: $45M Series D, $166M total funding. April 2026: launched construction intelligence category. 2026 customer additions: STO Building Group, Mortenson, Ledcor, Pomerleau. Prevents rework by catching discrepancies early.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with project management platforms; used by major GCs and owners globally
tools.spoken: Buildots has added major new construction firms to its customer roster in 2026, now including STO Building Group, Mortenson, Ledcor, and Pomerleau alongside existing clients Turner Construction, JE Dunn, Digital Realty, and Intel. The platform uses helmet-mounted cameras and computer vision to capture real-time site progress against the schedule. Total funding reached one hundred sixty-six million dollars on a forty-five million dollar Series D. Pricing is custom-quoted by project size.

tools.name: Raken
tools.description: Field documentation and daily reporting platform purpose-built for field crews on construction projects. Received a majority stake investment from Sverica Capital on September 9, 2025, providing new private equity backing for accelerated product development and market expansion. Now serves 4,500+ construction firms and 70,000+ users with daily reports, time tracking, toolbox talks, and production tracking.
tools.pricing: $15/user/month (Lite), $25/user/month (Pro); annual billing saves ~15%; 15-day free trial
tools.results: Sverica Capital majority investment September 2025. Serves 4,500+ construction firms and 70,000+ users. Reduces cost code entry time vs paper logs; real-time data sync from field to office.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, QuickBooks, major payroll platforms
tools.spoken: Raken received a majority stake investment from Sverica Capital in September 2025, giving the field reporting platform new private equity backing to accelerate product development and market expansion. Raken now serves more than forty-five hundred construction firms and seventy thousand users with daily reports, time tracking, toolbox talks, and production tracking. Pricing starts at fifteen dollars per user per month for the Lite plan and twenty-five dollars per user per month for Pro.

tools.name: BuildOps
tools.description: AI-native commercial contractor platform for specialty trades. Launched Projects module May 7, 2026, enabling multi-month construction work management from kickoff to closeout alongside service operations. Powered by OpsAI for scheduling, document management, field coordination, and daily reporting. Raised $127M Series C in 2026 reaching $1B valuation.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Used by 1,500+ specialty contractors. Projects module launched May 7, 2026. $127M Series C, $1B valuation in 2026.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with accounting and project management platforms
tools.spoken: BuildOps launched its Projects module in May 2026, giving specialty contractors an AI-native system to run multi-month construction work from kickoff to closeout alongside their service operations. Powered by OpsAI for scheduling, document management, field coordination, and daily reporting. BuildOps raised one hundred twenty-seven million dollars in a Series C round in 2026, reaching a one billion dollar valuation. Pricing is by custom quote.

tools.name: InspectMind AI
tools.description: Y Combinator-backed AI platform for construction field inspections and plan checking. Field Reports product captures site photos and multi-language voice notes on mobile, works offline, and generates structured daily reports, punch lists, and site walkthroughs using AI.
tools.pricing: $100/user/month (Field Reports); plan checking from $100/check or ~$1/drawing sheet at scale
tools.results: Generates field reports in minutes vs. hours; multi-language support including Spanish and Chinese to English; offline functionality for remote sites.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud
tools.spoken: InspectMind AI is a Y Combinator-backed platform that generates field reports, punch lists, safety inspections, and site walkthroughs in minutes. Its Field Reports product captures photos and voice notes on mobile, works offline, and produces structured reports at one hundred dollars per user per month. Plan checking starts at one hundred dollars per review. It integrates with Procore and Autodesk Construction Cloud.

tools.name: iFieldSmart
tools.description: Construction management platform with reality capture and AI-powered field documentation. Its Lens360 feature converts a single 360-degree site walkthrough into a complete digital twin with actionable insights, documentation, and progress tracking. Includes RFI and submittal tracking, task scheduling, and risk management. Agentic AI for construction risk detection in development.
tools.pricing: Starting at $14.99/user/month; free trial available; custom pricing for enterprise
tools.results: Single 360 walkthrough generates full site documentation and digital twin intelligence; reduces site visit time and documentation overhead.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, Airtasks, Assemble
tools.spoken: iFieldSmart from iFieldSmart Technologies is a construction management platform with reality capture and AI-powered field documentation. Its Lens three-sixty feature turns a single site walkthrough into a complete digital twin. Pricing starts at fourteen dollars and ninety-nine cents per user per month.

tools.name: Kahua
tools.description: Flexible, asset-centric construction management platform with built-in AI through its Noa assistant. Automates repetitive project workflows, uncovers insights from project data, and enables teams to build custom apps in minutes. Covers RFIs, submittals, punch lists, daily reports, and change orders in a single environment with automated routing and offline field capture.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; implementation costs typically $5,000-$50,000+ depending on scale
tools.results: Noa AI assistant enables project teams to build custom workflow apps in under 5 minutes; complete data ownership with real-time collaboration across all stakeholders.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with major accounting and project management platforms; API-based connectivity
tools.spoken: Kahua is a flexible construction management platform with built-in AI through its Noa assistant, which automates repetitive project workflows, uncovers insights from project data, and lets teams build custom apps in minutes. Pricing is custom-quoted with implementation costs typically ranging from five thousand to fifty thousand dollars or more.

tools.name: HCSS HeavyJob
tools.description: Construction project management and T&M billing platform that pulls billable work directly from field time cards to generate accurate T&M invoices. Expanded for utility contractor work order management end-to-end.
tools.pricing: $60-$4,000+/user/year depending on modules; custom enterprise pricing
tools.results: Go-to standard for heavy and civil specialty contractors on T&M work. Expanded for utility contractors at DISTRIBUTECH 2025.
tools.integrations: Major construction accounting systems
tools.spoken: HCSS HeavyJob is the go-to standard for heavy and civil specialty contractors doing time-and-material work. It pulls T and M billing data directly from field time cards and generates accurate invoices with minimal manual effort. Pricing ranges from sixty to over four thousand dollars per user per year depending on modules selected.

tools.name: FOUNDATION T&M Billing Module
tools.description: T&M billing module within FOUNDATION's construction accounting suite that captures all billable job expenses, automates markups, and produces professional invoices for time-and-material contracts.
tools.pricing: Custom quote as part of FOUNDATION accounting suite
tools.results: Produces precise T&M invoices; deep integration with construction accounting eliminates duplicate data entry.
tools.integrations: Native to FOUNDATION construction accounting; integrates with project management workflows
tools.spoken: FOUNDATION Software's T and M Billing Module captures every billable expense, automates markups, and produces client-ready invoices for time-and-material contracts. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: SmartBarrel
tools.description: AI biometric workforce management system combining facial recognition time tracking with PPE compliance monitoring at clock-in, validating each worker against a saved facial profile even in jobsite conditions.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on site count, worker count, and hardware deployment
tools.results: Eliminates buddy-punching; provides accurate T&M labor documentation; adds PPE compliance monitoring layer to time tracking hardware.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with payroll and project management systems
tools.spoken: SmartBarrel combines AI biometric facial recognition for time tracking with PPE compliance monitoring at clock-in, giving specialty subs a dual-purpose tool. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Rhumbix
tools.description: Mobile-first workforce tracking and T&M billing platform for construction that automates T&M ticket generation from field time card data, captures unplanned work and out-of-scope activities in real time, and produces streamlined change order documentation. Autodesk acquired Rhumbix in April 2026 to bring real-time jobsite labor and payroll data into its construction platform, connecting resource management with cost management, scheduling, estimating, and planning workflows. Rhumbix continues to operate its products and customer support without changes; integration with Autodesk Construction Cloud is planned.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on user count
tools.results: 50% reduction in T&M processing time; digital T&M tag processing reduced from 2-6 weeks to 1-2 days; cost overruns caught 2-3 weeks earlier than paper-based systems. Acquired by Autodesk April 2026; integration into Autodesk Construction Cloud underway.
tools.integrations: Autodesk Construction Cloud (acquisition integration in progress); Procore; QuickBooks Online Advanced; ADP Workforce Now
tools.spoken: Rhumbix is a mobile-first time and materials tracking platform that automates T and M ticket generation directly from field time cards and captures unplanned work in real time. It integrates with Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, QuickBooks Online, and ADP. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Siteline
tools.description: Construction billing and pay application platform built exclusively for commercial specialty subcontractors. Automates AIA pay app preparation, lien waiver collection, and vendor compliance tracking. Uses AI to validate waivers and flag errors before they delay payment. Has 23,000+ pre-built forms from 17,000+ GCs in the system.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on billing volume and integrations
tools.results: Customers bill 6x faster; 30% reduction in invoice aging; saves average of 7 days per week across billing workflow. 23,000+ GC forms in system. $18.4M total funding.
tools.integrations: Viewpoint Vista (two-way real-time sync), Procore Pay, Textura, GCPay, Foundation Software
tools.spoken: Siteline is the billing and pay application platform built exclusively for commercial specialty subcontractors. It automates AIA pay app preparation, lien waiver collection, and vendor compliance tracking. Customers bill six times faster and reduce invoice aging by thirty percent on average. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Adaptive
tools.description: AI-native construction accounting platform that deploys AI agents to automate manual accounting work, reconcile financials with field updates, and deliver real-time financial control. Agents continuously capture and code every cost to the correct job and cost code, build cost-plus and fixed-price billings from approved job costs, and monitor pending change requests to flag unapproved scope. Functions as an AI layer on top of QuickBooks.
tools.pricing: Starts at $575/month for businesses under $5M in annual revenue; revenue-based tiers above that threshold
tools.results: Automates 20+ hours of weekly accounting admin through AI agents; identifies overbilling, missing waivers, and unapproved scope; best suited for specialty trades doing $10M+ annually.
tools.integrations: QuickBooks (real-time two-way sync); API integrations with construction management platforms
tools.spoken: Adaptive is an AI-native construction accounting platform that deploys AI agents to automate manual accounting work and deliver real-time financial control. Pricing starts at five hundred seventy-five dollars per month for businesses with up to five million dollars in annual revenue.

tools.name: SubBase
tools.description: AI-powered materials procurement and invoice reconciliation platform for specialty subcontractors and self-performing GCs. Uses AI to automatically compare purchase orders, invoices, and delivery tickets for three-way reconciliation, completing the process in an average of 21 seconds vs. 3-5 minutes manually. Centralizes material management including vendor relationships, PO tracking, and delivery documentation. AI reconciliation feature has processed 4,000+ invoices covering $3.5M+ in purchases since launch.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: 21-second average AI reconciliation vs. 3-5 minutes manual. 4,000+ invoices processed since feature launch, covering $3.5M+ in purchases. Automates invoice approval to a single click after AI validation.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with accounting and ERP systems
tools.spoken: SubBase is an AI-powered materials procurement platform for specialty subcontractors that uses AI to automatically compare purchase orders, invoices, and delivery tickets for three-way reconciliation, completing the process in an average of twenty-one seconds compared to three to five minutes of manual work. Since the feature launched, SubBase has processed more than four thousand invoices covering over three and a half million dollars in purchases. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Payra
tools.description: B2B accounts receivable automation platform for construction contractors running on legacy ERP systems. Raised $15M from Edison Partners in February 2026. Integrates directly with Trimble Viewpoint, FOUNDATION Software, Sage, NetSuite, and others using proprietary AI-enabled technology without requiring ERP replacement. Automates cash application, payment matching, and reconciliation. Also integrates with ConstructCRM for end-to-end contractor payment acceleration.
tools.pricing: Transaction-based pricing (percentage of transaction volume); custom quote
tools.results: Customers document 20% reduction in DSO; 75% drop in past-due invoices. $15M Edison Partners investment February 2026. Automated cash application eliminates manual payment matching.
tools.integrations: Trimble Viewpoint, FOUNDATION Software, Sage, NetSuite; ConstructCRM partnership
tools.spoken: Payra is a B2B accounts receivable automation platform built for construction contractors running on legacy ERP systems, raising fifteen million dollars from Edison Partners in February 2026. It integrates directly with Trimble Viewpoint, FOUNDATION, Sage, and others to automate cash application and payment reconciliation without replacing existing systems. Customers have documented a twenty percent reduction in days sales outstanding and a seventy-five percent drop in past-due invoices. Pricing is transaction-based.

tools.name: Intuit Enterprise Suite Construction Edition
tools.description: AI-powered construction ERP launched February 11, 2026 as the first industry-specific edition of Intuit Enterprise Suite. Built for mid-market contractors. Includes project phases with AI-enabled cost group tracking (labor, materials, equipment, subcontractors), AIA-style invoicing, negative change orders, proposal builder with e-signatures, and an AI Project Management Agent that monitors cash flow, profitability, and project progress. Currently in open beta.
tools.pricing: No additional cost for existing Intuit Enterprise Suite customers (open beta); paid add-on for QuickBooks Online Advanced; Intuit may introduce fees in future
tools.results: First AI-powered construction-specific ERP from Intuit. AIA-style invoicing tracks contract value, invoiced to date, and remaining balance at phase level. Open beta at no extra cost since February 2026.
tools.integrations: QuickBooks Online Advanced; Intuit Enterprise Suite ecosystem
tools.spoken: Intuit launched its Enterprise Suite Construction Edition on February eleventh, 2026, delivering a purpose-built AI-powered ERP for mid-market contractors. It includes project phases with AI-enabled cost group tracking, AIA-style invoicing, negative change orders, proposal tools with e-signatures, and an AI Project Management Agent for cash flow and profitability monitoring. It is currently in open beta at no additional cost for existing Intuit Enterprise Suite customers, and available as a paid add-on for QuickBooks Online Advanced.

tools.name: Document Crunch
tools.company: Document Crunch (acquisition by Trimble announced April 2026, pending close)
tools.description: AI contract review and risk reduction platform that reads construction contracts, specs, and insurance documents and surfaces risk clauses, change order triggers, and compliance obligations in plain language. Trimble announced acquisition on April 2, 2026, with close expected Q2-Q3 2026 and planned integration into Trimble Construction One. Serves 400+ customers processing $350B+ in construction volume across 10,000 project kickoffs.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Reduces contract review time from hours to minutes. 400+ customers, $350B+ in construction volume, 10,000 project kickoffs. Trimble acquisition announced April 2, 2026; close expected Q2-Q3 2026.
tools.integrations: Trimble Construction One (integration planned post-acquisition close); prior Trimble ProjectSight integration
tools.spoken: Document Crunch is the AI contract review platform that reads construction contracts, specs, and insurance documents and surfaces risk clauses, change order triggers, and compliance obligations in plain language. Trimble announced its acquisition of Document Crunch on April 2, 2026, with the deal expected to close in the second quarter of 2026 and full integration planned into the Trimble Construction One ecosystem. The platform now serves more than four hundred customers processing three hundred fifty billion dollars in construction volume across ten thousand project kickoffs.

tools.name: Procore Change Orders with AI Assist
tools.description: AI-enhanced change order management within Procore platform that helps identify scope gaps, draft change order language, and track financial impacts in real time alongside the project schedule and budget.
tools.pricing: Included in Procore ACV-based subscription
tools.results: Contractors using AI estimating report 22% fewer change orders from estimating errors; real-time financial impact tracking reduces disputes.
tools.integrations: Native to Procore; integrates with Autodesk, Sage, Viewpoint, and 400+ tools
tools.spoken: Procore's change order management with AI Assist lets project teams identify scope gaps, draft change order language, and track financial impacts in real time. Pricing is on annual construction volume.

tools.name: Pelles AI
tools.description: Generative AI platform for construction that creates RFIs, submittal logs, proposals, and other construction documents in the team's existing format. Features DoubleCheck for surfacing requirements with clause-level citations, Compare for catching contradictions across drawings and specs, and AI workflows for auto-drafting RFIs and submittal registers.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Handles more document volume with greater confidence; enables small teams to compete on documentation quality with larger firms.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, SharePoint, Bluebeam
tools.spoken: Pelles AI is a generative AI platform that creates RFIs, submittal logs, and proposals in your company's existing format, giving smaller teams the output speed of a much larger document production operation. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Clearstory
tools.description: Change order and T&M management platform purpose-built for specialty contractors and GCs. In March 2026, Suffolk Construction named Clearstory the national standard for change order management across its entire portfolio, joining Turner Construction (350+ projects). New 2026 AI features: AI Reason Categories (automatically classifies every COR into root cause categories the moment it's created or received — now available in all accounts as of May 2026); Bulk AI Import (brings entire COR backlogs in with AI-driven field mapping). May 2026 product update simplifies COR readability when pulling from multiple T&M tags, making it easier for GC approvers to review and approve. New 2026 integrations: Sage Intacct Construction (direct sync) and Procore Contract Mapping (auto-populates contract, budget, and vendor info).
tools.pricing: Basic tier free for all stakeholders; Standard and Professional paid tiers add analytics, advanced reporting, and rate management; implementation $1,000-$5,000
tools.results: 75% faster COR approval; 70%+ reduction in time processing CORs. Turner Construction: 350+ projects. Suffolk Construction: named national standard March 2026. AI Reason Categories now available in all accounts (May 2026). May 2026: simplified COR line item display for multi-T&M-tag situations. New Sage Intacct and Procore Contract Mapping integrations.
tools.integrations: Procore (incl. Contract Mapping), Autodesk, CMiC, Sage, Sage Intacct, HCSS
tools.spoken: Clearstory has had a major 2026 with significant new deployments and product updates. Suffolk Construction named Clearstory the standard for change order management across its national portfolio in March 2026, joining Turner Construction which has deployed the platform on more than three hundred fifty projects. New AI features include AI Reason Categories, which automatically classifies change orders into root cause categories for change order intelligence; Bulk AI Import, which brings entire COR backlogs into the system with AI-driven field mapping; a new Sage Intacct integration; and Procore Contract Mapping for automatic contract data population. Specialty contractors get change orders approved up to seventy-five percent faster and cut processing time by seventy percent or more. The basic tier is free for all stakeholders.

tools.name: BidIntell
tools.description: AI pre-bid scoring platform that anchors proposal decisions by evaluating bid fit before any estimating labor is invested, using BidIndex scoring on location, contract terms, GC history, and trade match.
tools.pricing: $49/user/month
tools.results: Average bid preparation time industry-wide down from 34 hours to 14 hours per project for AI-using contractors (Dodge Construction Network 2025).
tools.integrations: Plans for estimating and takeoff tool integrations
tools.spoken: BidIntell's pre-bid scoring anchors the proposal phase, helping specialty subs invest estimating hours only on the invitations that fit their win profile before a single takeoff is started. Pricing starts at forty-nine dollars per user per month.

tools.name: Flowcase
tools.description: AI proposal management platform that matches company experience and employee qualifications to RFP requirements and auto-populates bid templates, with AI assistance for translation, proofreading, and content tailoring.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Strong for qualifications-based and design-assist competitions; reduces manual bid assembly time.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with project data systems
tools.spoken: Flowcase is an AI proposal management platform that matches company experience and employee qualifications to RFP requirements and auto-populates bid templates. Especially strong for design-assist and qualifications-based work. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: ContraVault
tools.description: AI pre-award workflow platform that takes teams from opportunity qualification through to submission-ready proposals with minimal manual effort, using AI to assemble, draft, and review proposal content.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Contractors using AI estimating and proposal tools report 19% fewer cost overruns and 22% fewer change orders from estimating errors (Dodge 2025). Average bid prep time down from 34 to 14 hours.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations
tools.spoken: ContraVault takes a team from opportunity qualification to a submission-ready proposal using AI to assemble, draft, and review content. Built for GC and specialty sub teams competing for complex awards. A 2025 Dodge Construction Network survey found that average bid preparation time has dropped from thirty-four hours to fourteen hours per project for contractors using AI-powered estimating, and those contractors reported nineteen percent fewer cost overruns and twenty-two percent fewer change orders traced to estimating errors.

tools.name: ALICE Technologies
tools.description: AI construction planning and scheduling platform that simulates millions of possible build sequences to find the most time and cost-efficient path. In April 2026, formalized a partnership with McKinsey and Company deploying generative scheduling across 35+ capital project clients in infrastructure, data centers, and energy, achieving schedule accelerations of up to 20%. Launched Schedule Insights Agent for natural language schedule queries.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: McKinsey data center client reduced schedule by 40%. Zachry Construction cut 28 days from a highway construction timeline. 17% reduction in project duration, 14% labor cost savings, 12% equipment cost savings. $32M in revenue protected on one hyperscale data center project. 35+ clients via McKinsey partnership.
tools.integrations: Oracle Primavera P6, Oracle Primavera Cloud, Autodesk; McKinsey alliance for enterprise capital project delivery
tools.spoken: ALICE Technologies formalized a partnership with McKinsey and Company in April 2026 to bring generative scheduling to capital project clients across infrastructure, data centers, and energy, introducing the technology to more than thirty-five clients with schedule accelerations of up to twenty percent. A McKinsey data center client reduced its entire construction schedule by forty percent. The platform's Schedule Insights Agent allows teams to interact with their schedule using natural language. Earlier documented results include a seventeen percent reduction in project duration, fourteen percent labor cost savings, and twelve percent equipment cost savings. Pricing is by custom quote.

tools.name: Phoenix Project Manager
tools.description: CPM scheduling software built for specialty contractors requiring resource-loaded critical path schedules without Primavera P6 complexity. Labor histogram view shows workforce demand across the full project portfolio.
tools.pricing: Starting at $1,800/year
tools.results: Purpose-built for electrical, mechanical, and plumbing contractors managing multi-project labor demand; significantly lower cost and learning curve than Primavera P6.

tools.spoken: Phoenix Project Manager is built for specialty contractors who need resource-loaded critical path schedules without the complexity of Primavera P six. Its labor histogram view lets electrical, mechanical, and plumbing contractors see workforce demand across their entire project portfolio. Starting at eighteen hundred dollars per year.

tools.name: Oracle Primavera P6
tools.description: Industry-standard CPM scheduling platform for large complex projects, with AI capabilities being extended through ALICE Technologies integration and Oracle's own Construction and Engineering Advisor suite.
tools.pricing: $2,150/user/year (on-premise); $2,570/year (cloud); 6-12 month learning curve
tools.results: Widely required by GCs and owners on large commercial and infrastructure projects; AI extended through ALICE integration.
tools.integrations: ALICE Technologies, Oracle Construction Suite, Procore, Autodesk
tools.spoken: Oracle Primavera P six remains the industry standard on large complex projects at roughly twenty-one hundred to twenty-five hundred dollars per user per year, and its AI capabilities are being extended through ALICE and third-party integrations.

tools.name: nPlan
tools.description: AI construction schedule forecasting and analytics platform trained on 750,000 historical schedules representing over $2 trillion of construction spend. Raised $16M Series B in October 2025 (total $36.5M). In February 2026, announced a partnership with the UK highways sector. In 2026, signed Anglian Water to use AI planning software for large-scale water infrastructure projects across its AMP8 investment window (2025-2030). Hosting Summer AI Day 2026 on June 25 themed 'Construction Superintelligence.' Products: Schedule Studio (generative AI schedules from scope docs), Barry (AI project decision assistant), nPlan Insights Pro (individual project forecasting), nPlan Portfolio (systemic risk and resource optimization).
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Schedule Studio turns scope docs into logic-linked schedules in minutes. February 2026: UK highways sector partnership. Anglian Water (AMP8, 2025-2030) signed 2026. Melbourne's $11B North East Link project adopted nPlan AI planning tools. $36.5M total funding.
tools.integrations: Oracle Primavera P6, Primavera Cloud, Microsoft Project
tools.spoken: nPlan announced a partnership with the UK highways sector in February 2026, further extending its AI scheduling capabilities trained on seven hundred fifty thousand historical schedules representing over two trillion dollars of construction spend. The company raised sixteen million dollars in a Series B in October 2025, bringing total funding to thirty-six and a half million dollars. Products include Schedule Studio, Barry the AI decision assistant, nPlan Insights Pro, and nPlan Portfolio. Pricing is by custom quote.

tools.name: SmartPM
tools.description: AI-driven automated project controls and schedule analytics platform that integrates with existing CPM scheduling software to automatically flag delays, variances, and critical path risks. Transforms raw schedule data into actionable dashboards and auto-generated reports. Total funding $13.5M with investors including GS Futures, Nemetschek Group, Sixth Sense, and Building Ventures.

tools.results: Predicts delays with 95% accuracy; automates advanced project controls analytics; real-time delay and variance flagging. $13.5M total funding.
tools.integrations: Autodesk Construction Cloud, Procore, Oracle Primavera P6
tools.spoken: SmartPM is an AI-driven automated project controls and schedule analytics platform that integrates with existing CPM scheduling software to flag delays and critical path risks in real time. Total funding is thirteen and a half million dollars. Pricing starts at four hundred dollars per project per month.

tools.name: Outbuild
tools.description: AI-enabled collaborative scheduling and field coordination platform. In April 2026, launched Project Intelligence, adding a mobile field app for foremen and superintendents, an AI Assistant for natural language schedule queries, and an executive dashboard for portfolio-wide visibility. Trusted by 400+ customers managing $50B+ in active projects.
tools.pricing: Scales by Annual Construction Volume; 30-day free trial; Enterprise plan available
tools.results: April 2026: Project Intelligence launch adds mobile field integration, AI assistant, and executive dashboard. 400+ customers with $50B+ active projects on platform.

tools.spoken: Outbuild launched Project Intelligence in April 2026, adding a mobile field app for superintendents and foremen, an AI Assistant for natural language schedule queries, and an executive dashboard for portfolio-wide visibility. Trusted by four hundred or more customers managing over fifty billion dollars in active projects. Pricing scales based on annual construction volume.

tools.name: Planera
tools.description: AI-powered CPM scheduling platform for general contractors and subcontractors using a visual canvas with project-based rather than per-user pricing. Raised $26.5M total funding including $8M in October 2025 to expand into data center construction after 600% growth in six months. AI schedule assistant identifies potential delays and acceleration opportunities.

tools.results: $26.5M total funding; 600% growth in 6 months; AI schedule assistant proactively identifies delays and acceleration opportunities.

tools.spoken: Planera is an AI-powered CPM scheduling platform with unlimited-user collaboration and project-based pricing. It raised twenty-six and a half million dollars in total funding and has experienced six hundred percent growth in six months. Pricing is project-based. Integrates with Oracle Primavera P six.

tools.name: Intenseye
tools.description: AI workplace safety platform using existing camera infrastructure to detect 50+ categories of unsafe acts and conditions in real time. Named World Economic Forum Global Innovator in February 2026. Integrated NVIDIA Cosmos Reason 2 in March 2026 to advance vision AI capabilities. Launched Intenseye Chief, an EHS-trained AI assistant. ~$94.4M total funding.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on camera count and facility size
tools.results: Companies report incident reductions of 40-50%. Named WEF Global Innovator February 2026. NVIDIA Cosmos Reason 2 integration March 2026. ~$94.4M total funding. Protects 25,000+ workers in 30+ countries.
tools.integrations: Works with existing camera infrastructure; custom integrations with safety management systems; Intenseye Chief AI assistant
tools.spoken: Intenseye uses existing camera infrastructure to detect more than fifty categories of unsafe acts and conditions in real time. Named a World Economic Forum Global Innovator in February 2026. Integrated NVIDIA Cosmos Reason 2 in March 2026 to advance vision AI capabilities and launched Intenseye Chief, an EHS-trained AI assistant that turns safety data into actionable insights. Total funding is approximately ninety-four million dollars. Pricing by custom quote based on camera count.

tools.name: Voxel AI
tools.description: AI-powered video analytics safety platform for workplace hazard detection and real-time safety event monitoring. Total funding $80M including a $44M Series B. Expanded to seven new countries including Malaysia, Vietnam, Mexico, Brazil, Chile, and Italy. Signed six Fortune 500 companies in 2025. Priced at 30-50% lower than comparable enterprise platforms. Leading a panel at ASSP Safety 2026 in June 2026.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; estimated 30-50% less than Intenseye; structured by camera count
tools.results: Signed six Fortune 500 companies in 2025. Expanded to 7 new countries. Practical entry point for smaller contractors; provides video evidence documentation of safety events. $80M total funding.

tools.spoken: Voxel AI offers AI-powered safety monitoring at an estimated thirty to fifty percent lower cost than comparable enterprise platforms. Total funding now at eighty million dollars including a forty-four million dollar Series B. Pricing structured around camera count.

tools.name: Oracle Construction and Engineering Advisor for Safety
tools.description: AI-enabled predictive safety intelligence solution launched in general availability March 5, 2026 that forecasts where safety incidents are likely to occur before they happen. Oracle-built industry-specific safety model trained on data spanning 10,000+ project-years. Incorporates AI vision capabilities from the Newmetrix (formerly Smartvid.io) asset acquisition completed in 2022.
tools.pricing: Custom quote as part of Oracle Construction and Engineering suite
tools.results: Shifts safety from reactive to proactive model; predictive incident identification before events occur. Trained on 10,000+ project-years of data. Bechtel deployed Detect Technologies PPE AI across 18,000-person craft workforce alongside Oracle safety tools.
tools.integrations: Oracle Construction and Engineering platform; Oracle Primavera
tools.spoken: Oracle launched its Construction and Engineering Advisor for Safety in March 2026, adding predictive analytics that forecast where safety incidents are likely before they happen. Built for large GC and owner teams running complex projects.

tools.name: Pillar Technologies
tools.description: Connected device and sensor platform for construction site risk management, combining environmental sensors (temperature, moisture, atmosphere) with visual monitoring for real-time hazard alerts and site risk intelligence.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Provides real-time multi-factor risk data; alerts teams to environmental conditions before they become safety incidents.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with project management and safety platforms
tools.spoken: Pillar Technologies uses connected environmental sensors alongside visual monitoring to deliver real-time construction site risk management, with alerts covering temperature, moisture, atmospheric conditions, and visual hazard detection. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: DroneDeploy Safety AI
tools.description: AI safety monitoring module within DroneDeploy's reality capture platform that analyzes drone imagery and 360-degree site camera data against all OSHA standards 1910 and 1926 to detect hazards including missing guardrails, exposed edges, and PPE violations. Reports visible safety risks at 95% accuracy.

tools.results: Beta customers reduced occurrence of unsafe conditions by up to 89% within 3 weeks; issues mapped to specific site locations and OSHA safety categories.

tools.spoken: DroneDeploy Safety AI analyzes drone imagery and site camera data against OSHA standards to detect construction hazards including missing guardrails, exposed edges, and PPE violations. Beta customers reduced the occurrence of unsafe conditions by up to eighty-nine percent within three weeks. Pricing for the individual plan starts at three hundred forty-nine dollars per month billed annually.

tools.name: viAct

Detects PPE violations, fall risks, unsafe acts at height, housekeeping hazards, restricted zone breaches, and worker-equipment proximity risks. Also functions as a digital EHS assistant automating incident reports and compliance documentation.
tools.pricing: $200/camera/module/month; 14-day free trial
tools.results: 200+ AI detection modules; automates EHS documentation and safety trend analysis.
tools.integrations: Existing camera infrastructure; custom EHS and safety management system integrations
tools.spoken: viAct transforms existing site cameras into AI safety monitors using more than two hundred specialized detection modules. Pricing starts at two hundred dollars per camera per module per month, with a fourteen-day free trial.

tools.name: Fyld

Raised $41M in a Series B round in February 2026. 82% year-over-year growth in 2025. Customers include Kiewit, Quanta Services, Emery Sapp and Sons, and Ferrovial. tools.results: 82% YoY growth in 2025. Up to 48% reduction in serious workplace incidents. Time to value as short as four weeks. $41M Series B raised February 2026. US revenue expected to exceed 40% of total by end of 2026.
tools.integrations: Mobile-first platform; custom integrations with field operations and safety management systems
tools.spoken: Fyld raised forty-one million dollars in a Series B round in February 2026 and reported eighty-two percent year-over-year growth in 2025. Contractors report up to forty-eight percent reduction in serious workplace incidents. Customers include Kiewit, Quanta Services, and Emery Sapp and Sons. Pricing is by custom quote.

tools.name: Safety Evolution SE-AI
tools.description: AI-powered EHS management platform with three dedicated AI agents replacing 20+ hours of weekly administrative safety work. The Compliance Monitor continuously tracks certifications, permits, and OSHA deadlines. The Workforce and Training Manager handles worker qualification documentation and training records. The Safety Intelligence agent transforms safety data into trend analysis and actionable recommendations. Includes built-in COR/SECOR compliance support and ISNetworld integration.
