tools.results: Replaces 20+ hours of weekly safety admin with three AI agents; automates certification tracking, training records, and OSHA compliance monitoring.
tools.integrations: ISNetworld; COR/SECOR compliance frameworks; custom integrations with HR and project management systems
tools.spoken: Safety Evolution's SE-AI platform replaces more than twenty hours of weekly administrative safety work with three AI agents covering compliance monitoring, workforce training management, and safety intelligence. It includes built-in support for COR and SECOR compliance and ISNetworld integration. Pricing is tiered per user.

tools.name: Kwant.ai
tools.description: AI-powered workforce management and safety platform combining ATEX-certified smart wearables, IoT sensors, and AI analytics. Smart badges with GPS and fall detection, environmental sensors, and biometric tracking connect via Bluetooth and LoRaWAN to deliver continuous workforce intelligence. Automates safety orientation, tracks certifications and licenses, verifies insurance documents, manages access control, and detects incidents in real time. Centralizes data from smart badges, sensors, and material and asset trackers on a unified dashboard.
tools.pricing: Starts at approximately $50,000 for single-site rollout (wearables + sensors); custom pricing for portfolio-level deployments; ROI typically 6-12 months
tools.results: Automates worker onboarding, certification tracking, and incident detection simultaneously. ATEX-certified hardware for hazardous environments. Single platform covers workforce management, safety compliance, and site intelligence.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with safety management, HR, and project management platforms; Bluetooth and LoRaWAN IoT connectivity
tools.spoken: Kwant dot AI is an AI-powered workforce management and safety platform that combines ATEX-certified smart wearables, IoT sensors, and AI analytics to automate safety orientation, track certifications, and detect incidents in real time. Pricing starts at approximately fifty thousand dollars for a single-site rollout including wearables and sensors, with custom pricing for portfolio deployments. It is built for both general contractors and specialty subcontractors.

tools.name: Hakimo AI
tools.description: NEW. AI-powered physical security and safety monitoring platform for construction jobsites. Uses existing camera infrastructure and AI to monitor for unauthorized access, safety condition violations, and incidents in real time. Skanska has deployed Hakimo AI to proactively monitor major infrastructure projects, preventing potential losses and ensuring safety compliance. Raised $10.5M Series A in 2025, bringing total funding to $20.5M. Particularly effective at reducing reliance on traditional security guard services while improving incident detection rates.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on camera count and site configuration
tools.results: Skanska deployment protecting major infrastructure projects. Customers documented 90% drop in incidents; security cost reductions of up to two-thirds vs. traditional guard services. $20.5M total funding ($10.5M Series A, 2025).
tools.integrations: Works with existing camera infrastructure; custom integrations with security and safety management platforms
tools.spoken: New in safety and compliance monitoring is Hakimo AI, an AI-powered physical security and safety monitoring platform for construction jobsites. Skanska has deployed Hakimo AI to proactively monitor major infrastructure projects, using AI-driven camera surveillance to prevent unauthorized access, detect safety conditions, and flag incidents in real time. Hakimo raised ten and a half million dollars in a Series A in 2025, bringing total funding to twenty and a half million dollars. Customers have documented a ninety percent drop in incidents and security cost reductions of up to two-thirds compared to traditional guard services. Pricing is by custom quote based on camera count.

tools.name: Sensera Systems
tools.description: NEW. AI-powered jobsite intelligence platform offering SiteCloud, a solar-powered, self-contained camera and software system that uses AI to monitor construction sites for safety hazards, security events, and project progress. Closed a $27M Series B funding round on February 26, 2026. AI continuously scans for OSHA Focus Four hazards (falls, struck-by, caught-in/between, electrocution), identifies workers without proper PPE, validates harness usage at height, and delivers real-time fall hazard alerts via text and email. SiteCloud Insights generates a daily Morning Brief summarizing site conditions, and Real-Time Fall Hazards can be exported directly into Procore Daily Logs with one click. Used by 2,000+ general contractors.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on camera count and site configuration; hardware and software bundled
tools.results: 2,000+ GC customers. $27M Series B closed February 26, 2026 (led by 10 Atlantic Group, Egis Capital Partners, MUUS Asset Management). Real-time OSHA Focus Four detection. TRITEC deployed SiteCloud on Long Island mixed-use hub. Daily Morning Brief and fall hazard alerts reduce need for constant site presence.
tools.integrations: Procore (direct export of Morning Brief into Daily Log notes; Real-Time Fall Hazards exported to safety violation notes in Procore Daily Log)
tools.spoken: Also new in safety and compliance monitoring is Sensera Systems, which closed a twenty-seven million dollar Series B in February 2026. Their SiteCloud platform uses solar-powered, self-contained cameras and AI to monitor jobsites for OSHA Focus Four hazards in real time, detect missing PPE, and validate harness use at height. The system delivers a daily Morning Brief and real-time fall hazard alerts that export directly into Procore Daily Logs with a single click. More than two thousand general contractors are already using Sensera. Pricing is by custom quote based on camera count.

market_context.owner_expectations: GCs increasingly mandate specialty subs manage submittals, RFIs, and documentation within Procore or Autodesk Build. BIM coordination and model-based submittals are standard on commercial projects over $10M. Digital daily reports with photo documentation and real-time cost tracking evolved from optional to contractual requirement. Subcontractors unable to engage digitally face elimination from bid lists before initial contact. "AI adoption in construction has now doubled year over year, with thirty-eight percent of contractors reporting measurable business impact from AI, up from seventeen percent just one year ago, and ninety-four percent of those using AI plan to increase their usage in 2026."
market_context.adoption_cost: A functional AI toolkit for specialty subs managing 5-20 project managers typically costs $15,000-$80,000 annually in software. Bundled platform selection versus best-of-breed point solutions drives cost variance significantly. Training adds 15-20% to total investment and remains systematically underestimated. Full operational efficiency emerges within 60-90 days post-implementation.
market_context.roi: Early-adopter data demonstrates compelling returns. "A 2025 Bluebeam industry report found that sixty-eight percent of AI early adopters in construction have saved at least fifty thousand dollars, and forty-six percent have recovered between five hundred and one thousand hours of labor." The average return across the industry is 3.5x the investment, with top performers reporting 10x ROI. Bid preparation time is down 60% maximum. Clark Construction documented $3.2M in project savings through AI-assisted risk management. Firms using AI scheduling report 20-30% fewer project delays. "Payra customers using AI accounts receivable automation document a twenty percent reduction in days sales outstanding and a seventy-five percent drop in past-due invoices."
